title: digitaria cognata subsp. pubiflora
intro: Digitaria cognata subsp. pubiflora, also known as the pubic-flowered finger grass, is a type of grass native to tropical Africa. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to treat fever, headache, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to prevent the spread of malaria. In herbal preparations, Digitaria cognata subsp. pubiflora is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions to harness its medicinal properties.
